data: Group Discussion - Resume — Does It Truly Reflect a Candidate
Group Discussion - Resume — Does It Truly Reflect a Candidate
Learn how to discuss resume in a group discussion

message:
Hey! Today we're learning how to discuss Resume — Does It Truly Reflect a Candidate in a group discussion.

Using the right structure helps you speak clearly and confidently.
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/ac97b412-5884-4fb6-82c6-42597079df71-GD---v2-(1).jpg 
button_text: Cool!

message:
When you want to talk about what something offers or contributes, use this structure:

"[Topic] offers [advantage]."

For example: "A resume offers a quick overview of the candidate's profile."
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/ac97b412-5884-4fb6-82c6-42597079df71-GD---v2-(1).jpg 
button_text: Got it!

message:
Here's another example.

If someone asks about the benefits of a resume, you can say:

"A resume offers clarity and structure to the recruitment process."
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/ac97b412-5884-4fb6-82c6-42597079df71-GD---v2-(1).jpg 
button_text: Next!

message:
When you want to mention both the advantage and the drawback, use this structure:

"While [topic] offers [advantage], it also has a [drawback]."

For example: "While a resume offers basic screening, it also has a risk of missing true talent."
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/ac97b412-5884-4fb6-82c6-42597079df71-GD---v2-(1).jpg 
button_text: Makes sense!

message:
Let's practice discussing the role of a resume!

image_url:
button_text: Start Practice!

Select the correct way to talk about what a resume offers

1.A resume offers a quick overview of the candidate's profile.
2.A resume is not important in the hiring process.

Correct answer: 1

Select the correct way to talk about the drawback of a resume

1.Resumes are always accurate and should be fully trusted.
2.While a resume offers organised information, it also has the challenge of being inaccurate.

Correct answer: 2
A resume offers recruiters a way to quickly identify the most suitable candidates.
While a resume offers a structured format, it also has the challenge of not capturing personality.
A resume offers a platform for candidates to showcase their key achievements.
While a resume offers standardized information, it also has the risk of creating bias in selection.
A resume offers candidates a professional way to make a strong first impression.

first_message: That was incredible! Now we're going to practice by answering few questions in English. Can we start?
prompt_id: 4d3f5213-fcc4-40f3-879d-051908377a31
questions: "'1. What does a resume offer to a recruiter? Use Structure 1.'

'2. While a resume offers a first impression, what challenge does it also bring? Respond using Structure 2.'

'3. What does a resume offer to the hiring process? Use Structure 1.'

'4. While a resume offers documented skills, what risk does it also bring? Give your answer using Structure 2.'

'5. What does a resume offer to a candidate's job search? Use Structure 1.'"
